items.title: State argument order explicitly
items.avoid: Writing "KL between P and Q" without arrows or sampling distribution.
items.prefer: Write D_KL(P || Q) and note "expectation over X ~ P" in papers, code comments, and loss definitions.
items.why: Prevents accidental swap of forward and reverse KL in objectives with different optimization behavior.
items.example: In a VAE loss comment: `# KL(q(z|x) || p(z)) — sample z from q, compare log q to log p`.

items.title: Handle zero probabilities safely
items.avoid: Raw log(q) when q can hit exact zeros on the support of p.
items.prefer: Use log-softmax outputs, label smoothing, or masked support; treat true support mismatch as a bug to fix.
items.why: Numerical zeros in q where p > 0 produce undefined or misleading KL values.
items.example: Prefer `F.log_softmax(logits)` + `F.kl_div` over `log(softmax)` with hard zeros in q.

items.title: Match log base and units across terms
items.avoid: Adding a KL term in nats to a cross-entropy reported in bits without conversion.
items.prefer: Pick one base (usually nats in PyTorch) and convert consistently when combining or reporting losses.
items.why: KL values are only comparable across runs when units align; mixing bases scales all thresholds incorrectly.
items.example: Multiply bit-based KL by ln(2) ≈ 0.693 before adding to PyTorch nats loss.
